mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal fails to predict downturns in historical labor cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth outpaces the industrial production index, it indicates robust aggregate demand absorbing supply capacity, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 growth minus INDPRO growth) shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with forward returns across three consecutive rolling quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 320, persistent inflation expectations force market repricing of discount rates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL sustains values above 320 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household disposable income and consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS month-over-month changes falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant premium during payroll growth streaks.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in residential construction activity below 1200 thousand units signals tightening credit conditions and weakening consumer confidence, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced economic activity and earnings revisions.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when HOUST drops below 1200, or the signal fails to predict downturns in historical housing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of elevated federal funds rates and rising unemployment creates a stagflationary shock that compresses equity valuations and earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of FEDFUNDS and UNRATE changes shows no predictive power for forward returns, or equity markets rally despite both variables moving in the adverse direction.

mechanism_narrative: When total consumer credit balances exceed 750 billion dollars, it reflects robust household borrowing and spending momentum that directly supports corporate sales, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings estimates are revised upward.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative or statistically insignificant when RSAFS breaches the threshold, or credit growth decouples from equity performance during historical credit booms.
